wikitext:
The '''[[S&P 500]]''' [[stock market index]], maintained by [[Standard & Poor's]], comprises 500 [[market capitalization|large-cap]] American companies covering about 75% of the American equity market by capitalization.  The index components listed below are current as of the start of the trading day of March 9, 2011.

==Recent changes to the list of S&P 500 Components==



<!--                                                EDITORS PLEASE TAKE NOTE:

 If you are updating the following table due to a change which occurred in the components of the S&P 500 please consider the following.

 1.  S&P never does adds or removes a stock in isolation, it's always "AAA replaces BBB".  
     Please do not simply add or remove a component;  Rather, make sure you record both parts of the change.  

 2.  If you record a change here, please also make the corresponding changes in the above table "S&P 500 Component Stocks"
     Keeping these two tables in sync should make it much easier for editors to keep track of which changes have/haven't been entered.

     Thanks!                       -->



Standard & Poors updates the components of the S&P 500 periodically, typically in response to acquisitions, or to keep the index up to date as various companies grow or shrink in value.
